title: Samuel Raider
content:
{{Top|pfa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Samuel}} {{FAreview}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name=Samuel Raider |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Dark |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] **[[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]][[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I've never had too much trouble with them, though, unless the odds were about ten to one.''"
"''I always figured ten to one was pretty routine.''"
"''I was just trying to be modest. Hope I get to fly with you some time, captain.''"
"''Call me Sam.|Biggs Darklighter and Samuel Raider discuss the TIE/IN interceptor|}} '''Samuel Raider''' was a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After he was captured by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Raider was sent to [[Stars' End/Legends|Stars' End]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]], but was able to escape with the help of other captive [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]]. Fleeing to [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the escapees were later [[Rescue from Stars' End|recovered]] by Rebel forces, though Raider's friend [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Cardacs]] was killed during the rescue. Raider joined [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] aboard the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 ''Home One'' type Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], bringing news of a secret Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|weapon]] with him. He initially blamed Cardacs' [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]], [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], for his friend's death, but the two went on to become friends themselves. During his time with Red Squadron, Raider fought in several missions against the Empire as the Rebels attempted to learn more information about the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], including the [[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)|destruction]] of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Intrepid (Imperial I-class)|''Intrepid'']]. However, his relationship with Farlander was strained when Farlander's instructions contributed to the deaths of two rookie pilots. When the Death Star moved to [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|attack]] [[Great Temple/Legends|Yavin Base]], Raider was one of several ''Independence'' [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] transferred to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] for the upcoming battle, and was among the few to survive. ==Biography== ===Capture=== Samuel Raider joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and rose to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]]. Some time before the end of [[Operation Strike Fear]], he was captured by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and sent to the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority's]] infamous [[Stars' End/Legends|Stars' End]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on [[Mytus VII/Legends|Mytus VII]]. During his time in the prison, Raider heard rumors of a secret Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|weapon]] under construction but was unable to get close enough to anybody who had been there to learn more. He was eventually able to escape, along with several other captured Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], and stowed away aboard the [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighter]] ''[[Genue]]'' bound for the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. [[File:Genue.jpg|thumb|left|260px|Rebel forces mount a rescue of Raider aboard the ''Genue''.]] The Empire quickly began searching all [[Starship/Legends|ships]] in the surrounding area in a bid to locate the fugitives. When the ''Genue'' arrived in the [[Kashyyyk system/Legends|Kashyyyk system]], it was stopped by the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Intrepid (Imperial I-class)|''Intrepid'']], which dispatched [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] aboard a [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|''Delta''-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport]] to board and search the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]. However, the Rebel Alliance had learned of the prisoners' escape and launched a mission to [[Rescue from Stars' End|rescue]] the prisoners before they could be recaptured. Captain [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] and an old friend of Raider's, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Cardacs]], flew [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearhead]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] from [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] into the Kashyyyk system, arriving just as the Imperial [[Transport/Legends|transport]] began docking with the freighter. The ''Intrepid''{{'s}} [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighters]] moved to intercept the threat, but while Cardacs held off the fighters, Farlander moved quickly to destroy the transport with his [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]]. As the Rebel [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] ''[[Drago]]'' arrived and began boarding, the ''Intrepid'' launched fighters to stop the rescue attempt, but the R-22s were able to hold them off long enough for the rescue team to capture the freighter and for both the ''Genue'' and the ''Drago'' to escape into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Cardacs, however, was killed during the [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]]. Raider and the other escapees were taken to the Rebel [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 ''Home One'' type Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], where they underwent an extensive debriefing. The following day, Raider was taking a break in the ship's mess with two of the other former prisoners, when he saw Farlander enter the room. Raider confronted the pilot, making it clear that he blamed Farlander for Cardacs' death. ===Wookiee rescue mission=== {{Quote|Let me ask you something, Captain Raider. Did you choose me for this mission to check me out, or to shoot me in the back?''"
"''To check you out. Yeah. But to shoot you? What kind of pilot do you think I am, anyway?''"
"''A pretty good one, from what I saw. But you were…you seemed to blame me.''"
"''I was wrong. Let's leave it at that. Come on. I've got some drinking to do, and I need some company.|Keyan Farlander and Samuel Raider|}} [[File:KeyanFarlander.png|left|thumb|Raider came into conflict with fellow pilot Keyan Farlander (pictured).]] Raider encountered Farlander again during a mission briefing the next day. During their debriefing, the escapees had informed the Rebels of the rumors regarding the secret Imperial project. Though they knew little, they had learned that a freighter was departing Kashyyyk, transporting Wookiee [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] to work on the project. The Alliance decided to attempt to [[Rescue of enslaved Wookiees|rescue]] the Wookiees and hoped that they would know more about their destination—Raider was chosen to lead the rescue mission. The ''Independence''{{'s}} mission planner, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[H. S'man|S'man]], introduced Raider to the assembled pilots, and the captain outlined the mission. In order to check Farlander out, Raider picked him, along with [[Sunnar Jan-lo]], as his wingmen. However, when his fellow escapee [[Toalagar]] voiced a desire to get back into action, Raider decided to take the other pilot instead of Jan-lo. The three Rebel pilots flew R-22 Spearheads in the mission. Upon dropping out of hyperspace, the group quickly came under attack from the Empire's new starfighter, the [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboat]]. During the subsequent dogfight, Farlander and Raider attacked the same target, and Raider's [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] fire raked Farlander's [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]]. Raider then admonished Farlander over his choice of target. The Assault Gunboats were soon eliminated, though Toalagar's R-22 was destroyed by a concussion missile launched by one of the Assault Gunboats during the first pass, and the pilot was killed. Nevertheless, Raider and Farlander were successful in engaging the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] escorting the freighter [[Toral (BFF-1 bulk freighter)|''Toral'']] and confirming the freighter's cargo. With their target confirmed, Rebel [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] arrived to disable it for capture by a Rebel transport. Raider and Farlander engaged reinforcements from the nearby ''Intrepid'' until the transport escaped with the freed Wookiees. Raider blamed himself for Toalagar's death, realizing that his friend was too rusty to have returned to combat immediately. After returning to the ''Independence'', he approached Farlander to apologize for both the friendly fire and blaming him for Cardacs' death. When Farlander was later grounded pending an investigation into the deaths of his two wingmen, [[Tan'tro]] and [[Ahsmar]], during a [[Battle of Orron III|mission]], Raider joined Commander [[Hamo Blastwell]] in reassuring Farlander of the other pilots' support. The three pilots went on to become friends, spending much of their time together in the pilots' lounge while not on duty. After pilots from the ''Independence'' [[Rescue of Biggs Darklighter|rescued]] [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] when his X-wing, which was carrying [[Death Star plans/Legends|information]] on the Death Star, broke down near the [[Cron Drift/Legends|Cron Drift]], Darklighter joined Raider and Farlander in the lounge, and the pilots shared their experiences. === Circarpous system raids === {{Quote|I figure ten-to-one is pretty routine."
"I was just trying to be modest. Hope I get to fly with you some time, Captain.
"Call me Sam.|Samuel Raider and Biggs Darklighter|}}[[File:Intrepid.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Alliance forces battle the ''Intrepid''.]]Soon after, the ''Intrepid''{{'s}} [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] was damaged in an accidental collision with an [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]], leaving the ship stranded in the [[Circarpous system/Legends|Circarpous system]]. In a series of [[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)|raids]], the Alliance prevented the Imperials from conducting repairs and eliminated its escorts, leaving it defenseless and vulnerable to attack. On one of these raids, Raider joined with Farlander, Blastwell, and [[Ernek Marskan]] in a mission to destroy the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer's]] escort of Assault Gunboats and [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] from [[Viper (corvette group)|''Viper'']] group. Soon after, Raider participated in the final assault on the ''Intrepid'', flying a [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]] R-22 Spearhead in the combined [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]], Y-wing and R-22 attack. Farlander and Jan-lo quickly brought down the Star Destroyer's shields with their X-wings, allowing the Y-wings' [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] barrage to eliminate the vessel, the first time the Rebels destroyed a Star Destroyer in a frontal attack. Although Jan-lo's X-wing was incapacitated by a [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bomber]] of [[Gamma Squadron/Legends|Gamma Squadron]], Raider quickly destroyed the bomber and Jan-lo was rescued. Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Raider, Farlander, and their fellow pilot [[Puck Naeco/Legends|Puck Naeco]] witnessed the arrival of the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] and [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[Tuz]] aboard the ''Independence'' when he made a [[Capture of the Sidral II|delivery]] of stolen Imperial equipment. Although Raider was suspicious of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]], due to some unknown prior dealings, he agreed to join Tuz's party for a drink in the pilots' lounge. When Raider voiced his opinion of Tuz's business practices, one of the pirate's [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] bodyguards attacked the pilot. Raider quickly ducked out of the way and responded with an uppercut to the Gamorrean's jaw, knocking the bodyguard to the floor and injuring his own hand in the process. The altercation was brought to a halt by Tuz, who admonished his bodyguard's behavior, but Raider's suspicions about Tuz's motives were confirmed when he learned that the pirate was only helping the Alliance as an unwilling participant in an arrangement with the ''Independence''{{'s}} commanding officer, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]]. ===Falling out with Farlander=== {{Quote|Leave me alone, Farlander. I don't know what you're up to, but I don't want anything to do with it.|Samuel Raider to Keyan Farlander|}} Raider and Naeco later joined Farlander and his rookie wingman [[Breth Gart]] in [[Lucky Lower Thirteen]], a bar aboard ship. The group soon attracted two female crew members, one of whom was clearly interested in Gart. When the young pilot decided to follow Farlander's lead in retiring for the night, the women stayed in the company of Raider and Naeco. When Gart was killed in a freak incident during a [[Battle of Kalla VII|mission]] soon after, Farlander took his death hard. Concerned, Raider approached Farlander to offer his condolences and was surprised when the other replied that Gart simply hadn't been strong enough to survive. As Farlander started to behave increasingly aggressive during combat, his friendship with Raider became strained. On one occasion, as Farlander entered the pilots' lounge, Raider stood up to leave. Farlander attempted to make his friend stay by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Mind trick/Legends|persuade]] him, but although Raider hesitated momentarily, he continued towards the exit, causing the frustrated Farlander to unleash a [[Telekinesis/Legends|mind blast]]. Raider staggered from the attack but soon straightened himself and warned Farlander to stay away from him. [[File:Battle_of_Yavin.png|thumb|left|260px|Alliance starfighters approach the Death Star.]] Around the same time, Farlander began promoting aggression in some of the ship's rookie pilots, hoping to create an invincible [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]], guided by the Force—his teachings contributed to the deaths of two of his followers, [[Horodi]] and [[Casal Marskan]], during his next [[Skirmish in the Horuz system|mission]]. After returning to the ''Independence'', Farlander was confronted by Casal's older brother, Captain Ernek Marskan, who blamed Farlander for his brother's death. Farlander used the Force to choke Marskan, before finally releasing him. Marskan reported the event to Raider and [[Speedy]], only serving to deepen the growing distrust of Farlander among his fellow pilots. Shortly after the incident, the ''Independence'' was dispatched to the Alliance's [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to assist in the preparations for an expected attack from the now complete Death Star. Raider was one of several pilots transferred to Yavin Base upon arrival, along with Marskan and Farlander. Since their confrontation on the ''Independence'', Farlander had come to realize the dangers of using the Force and turned his back on the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. After the arrival of the Death Star in the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]], Farlander approached his followers in the hangar and told them that he had been wrong. Raider watched from across the hangar but returned to his work without speaking to Farlander. The Rebels succeeded in defeating the Imperial [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] due to the efforts of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Though many Rebels were killed in the Battle of Yavin, Raider, Marskan, and Farlander were among the survivors. However, despite Farlander's apology, neither Raider nor Marskan were willing to forgive him. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Hey, to me an explosion is an explosion.|Samuel Raider|}} Raider was loyal to both the cause of the Rebel Alliance and his comrades. However, to those he disliked, such as the pirate Tuz, he was hard-edged and dangerous. Keyan Farlander noted Raider's firm grip and unwavering eyes while shaking hands during an early meeting between the two. Raider was unafraid of physical confrontations and was capable of holding his own in a fight, even against a Gamorrean on one occasion. Raider was a capable pilot who was confident of his own abilities. He considered [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]] to be easy to defeat, as their [[TIE pilot|pilots]] tended to rely too heavily on speed, and enjoyed the challenge of fighting CR90 corvettes. He once stated his desire to fight directly against a Star Destroyer—eventually getting the opportunity in the attack on the ''Intrepid''. In battle, he enjoyed using the advantage of deflector shields by ramming the unshielded Imperial TIE fighters. ==Behind the scenes== Samuel Raider was first identified in [[Rusel DeMaria]]'s book ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', which was published on [[June 1]], [[1993]]. The book presented the story of Keyan Farlander, the player character in the game [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], which was released on [[February 28]], 1993. DeMaria's book established one of the previously unidentified members of Farlander's squadron in the game to be Raider. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] {{1st}} {{C|Fighter only}} ==Sources== *''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' {{1stID|Samuel Raider}} *''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Raider, Samuel}} [[Category:Gold Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Red Squadron personnel]]
